Secondary Endpoint(s).value: Failure-free survival (FFS), Progression-free survival (PFS), Metastatic progression-free survival (MPFS), Freedom from symptomatic skeletal events, Prostate cancer-specific survival (Death from prostate cancer), Death from other causes.
Secondary Endpoint(s).reasoning: The 'Trial design' section on page 3 states: 'Failure-free survival (FFS) was an intermediate primary OM, defined as time from randomisation to the first of: rising PSA... new disease or progression of: distant metastases, lymph nodes or local disease; or death from prostate cancer. Progression-free survival (PFS) was defined as time from randomisation to the first of: new disease or progression of: distant metastases, lymph nodes or local disease; or death from prostate cancer [15]. Metastatic PFS (MPFS) was defined as time from randomisation to death from any cause, new metastases or progression of distant metastases.' The 'Conclusions' in the 'Results' section on page 2 mentions 'prostate cancer-specific survival, nor in other important outcomes such as symptomatic skeletal events.' Table 2 on page 7, titled 'Hazard ratio for SOC + AAP relative to SOC + DocP from adjusted Cox models', lists these outcomes under 'Outcome measure' including 'Failure-free survival', 'Progression-free survival', 'Metastatic progression-free survival', 'Freedom from symptomatic skeletal events', 'Death from prostate cancer', and 'Death from other causes', in addition to 'Overall survival' which is the primary endpoint.

Type of Therapy.value: The clinical trial provided two main interventions in addition to standard-of-care (SOC) long-term hormone therapy for prostate cancer: 1. SOC + docetaxel: docetaxel (75 mg/m² 3-weekly×6) plus prednisolone (10 mg daily). 2. SOC + abiraterone acetate: abiraterone acetate (1000 mg) plus prednisolone (5 mg daily). The standard-of-care (SOC) itself consisted of long-term androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) with LHRH analogues or orchidectomy, with mandated or encouraged radiotherapy depending on disease stage.

The 'Method' section on page 1 and 'Trial treatment, masking and follow-up' section on page 3 further detail the treatments: 'SOC was long-term ADT or, for most non-metastatic cases, ADT for ≥2 years and RT to the primary tumour.' and 'SOC + docetaxel 75 mg/m² 3-weekly×6 + prednisolone 10 mg daily; or SOC + abiraterone acetate 1000 mg + prednisolone 5 mg daily.' The SOC is further described as 'long-term hormone therapy with LHRH analogues (with short term antiandrogen if relevant) or orchidectomy' and radiotherapy.
